regimeSub: June NFP shock (57K vs ~150K est) collides with Warsh's hawkish Sintra posture; growth scare meets sticky 4.2% CPI.
themes: June NFP printed 57K vs ~150K expected, labor force participation at 5-year low → stagflation fears intensify as Warsh's July hike threat meets crumbling labor demand
Fed Chair Warsh at Sintra (Jul 1) vowed to 'disappoint' inflation doves, rate decision in 4 weeks → DXY softened to 100.8 post-payrolls as hike odds compress
BOJ at 1% with tapering end in FY2027 fails to arrest USDJPY at 161.15 → real rate gap persists as Japan fiscal concerns steepen curve despite nominal tightening
SK Hynix market cap flip over Samsung on HBM dominance continues → KOSPI 7855 holds but KRW stabilizes at 1539 after 'Sell Korea' rout exhaustion

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 2y 4.17% vs CPI 4.2% → real rate -3bps; policy effectively neutral despite Warsh hawkish rhetoric

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 31bps steepening (4.17% to 4.48%) reflects growth slowdown fears post-NFP shock, not rate cut bets

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: VIX at 16.15 suggests credit complacent; watch for spread widening if payrolls weakness persists

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.15 subdued despite NFP miss; holiday-thinned markets masking true vol repricing risk

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: 72.18 off April/May Iran war highs; Hormuz reopening narrative easing energy-driven inflation fears

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: 4194.9 rebounding from worst monthly drop since 2008 as NFP miss softens USD, real rates compress

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1539 stabilizing after 1550 breach; foreign outflows pausing but structural 'Sell Korea' overhang persists

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: SK Hynix > Samsung on HBM; AI capex thesis intact (TSMC raised guidance) but memory cycle risk flagged

headline: June payrolls shock (57K) injects stagflation doubt as Warsh's hawkish Sintra stance meets collapsing labor momentum, forcing markets to reprice July FOMC odds.
